steps.title: Apply the decay factor
steps.content: A decrease of $80\%$ leaves $20\%$ of the previous value, so each increase of $1$ in $x$ multiplies the value by $0.2$.

steps.title: Evaluate after two changes
steps.content:
Starting from $f(0)=89$, multiply by $0.2$ twice to reach $f(2)$.
$$f(2)=89(0.2)^2=89(0.04)=3.56$$
The answer is 3.56.
